label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what Steve Rowson thinks of the newer football helmets. The options are A to D. First, I need to find the part of the text where Steve Rowson is mentioned. The article says that Steve Rowson is an engineer at Virginia Tech who tests helmets. The relevant part is: "No helmet can prevent concussions completely, says Rowson. But he's found that when wearing a newer football helmet, the player's head doesn't gain speed as much after a hit. That lowers the risk of concussions." So, the key point here is that newer helmets reduce the speed gain of the head after a hit, which in turn lowers concussion risk. Now, looking at the answer choices: A) They vary a lot in size. The text doesn't mention anything about the size variation of the helmets. So probably not A. B) They work better than the old ones. The text says that newer helmets make the head not gain as much speed, implying improvement over older ones. So B seems possible. C) They slow down the player's running speed. The text talks about the head's speed after a hit, not the player's overall running speed. So C is incorrect. D) They allow the players to move their heads more
